theme: one rule paints the whole mural
title: One Rule Paints the Whole Mural
seed_problem: reproduce a target binary image with a cellular automaton
mutation: composite: couple seed-optimization (choose a bounded sparse initial configuration inside a small central box) with the choice of an outer-totalistic CA rule table, scored by target-overlap (true-positive minus false-positive) after a fixed number of steps; the box bound plus the seed budget force most of the picture to be GENERATED by the rule's dynamics rather than stored in the seed.
innovation_hook: With a bounded sparse seed, the pattern must be produced by the rule's dynamics, not memorized in the initial state.
strategies: trivial: identity rule (cells never change) with <=K seed cells placed on target pixels inside the box -> reproduces exactly the checker baseline B (ratio 0.1); can never touch the >=35% of the mural outside the box

greedy (the trap): seed the K densest target pixels in the box, then try a few FAMOUS rules {identity, Conway B3/S23, fill B3/S012345678} and keep the best-overlapping -- fills blobby regions a bit but its fixed rules cannot reproduce exotic-dynamics targets and it never optimizes the seed

insight (strong): treat it as inverse morphogenesis -- screen a LIBRARY of outer-totalistic rules (including replicator/seeds/gnarl/34-life/diamoeba), then for the best rules run forward-simulation seed optimization (greedy forward-selection + local toggles inside the box) so a sparse seed self-organizes into the mural, covering cells outside the box the seeding strategies cannot reach
beyond strong: co-search rule bits and seed jointly (simulated annealing / gradient-free search over the 18 rule bits and seed layout) toward exact inversion
